images.altText: Two security officers march a man away from a table
US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the suspected shooter on social media.

articles.title: Trump was 'all set to really rip', but shooting has changed tune on media

children.content: While the latest act of violence wasn't as close as Trump's brush with death in Butler, Pennsylvania, almost two years ago, it will no doubt revive some trauma for Trump and those closest to him.

image.altText: Trump in a tuxedo

articles.title: Trump says shooter's 'manifesto' shows he is a 'sick guy'

children.content: Several US media outlets are reporting on the existence of the manifesto, which reportedly states the shooter wanted to target administration officials and was sent to the suspect's family members just before the attack.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the suspected shooter on social media.

children.content: US Acting Attorney-General Todd Blanche says the shooter apprehended at the White House correspondents' dinner was likely targeting Donald Trump. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says he 'wanted to see' as gunshots rang out and 'probably' made Secret Service act more slowly

updates.title: Donald Trump speaks to 60 Minutes in US
Former US president Barack Obama commends Secret Service
White House: 'This political violence has to end'

items.title: Trump was being shown a party trick. Then gunshots rang out

children.content: Thousands of influential players in Washington filed into a ballroom with the US president. Then shots rang out.

image.altText: a composite image of a female security service agent with gun in front of satellite image of Washington DC

items.title: King Charles heads to US for Trump state visit after shooting incident

children.content: The British monarch and Queen Camilla are embarking on a state visit to the United States with relations between the two countries strained and following a shooting at a press gala in Washington.

image.altText: Two men holding wine glasses lean toward each other in conversation

items.title: Australia's fugitives found sanctuary in Istanbul. Then it all fell apart

children.content: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

gallery.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

items.title: Two children believed unaccounted for in Hawkesbury house fire

children.content: Two children are unaccounted for, the ABC understands, after five people were rescued from a house fire in Bowen Mountain on Monday morning in the Hawkesbury region.

image.altText: Bowen Mountain heli shot

items.title: Search for Alice Springs 5yo continues after no sighting overnight

children.content: Police resume daylight foot patrols after overnight searches failed to find Alice Springs five-year-old girl Sharon.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: A fascinating admission reveals truth beneath the chest beating in this war

children.content: It's a ceasefire in name only between Israel and its targets in Lebanon, as the wait continues for more talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran war drags on

children.content: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

image.altText: A grey frigate is seen in the foreground, sailing in Sydney Harbour, with the bridge to the right of the picture.

articles.title: Israel demands residents evacuate villages in southern Lebanon

children.content: Israeli forces have demanded residents in seven villages north of Lebanon's Litani River to evacuate.

image.altText: Several trees surrounding an army vehicle driving through the dirt.

gallery.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

thumbnail.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Russia for meeting with Vladimir Putin

children.content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will meet Russian President Vladimir Putin during a visit to Moscow later today, the country's ISNA news agency reports. Follow live.

image.altText: An older man with short, grey hair and glasses speaks in front of a grey background.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Maccas wins battle to set up in 'world's coolest street'

children.content: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

gallery.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.

thumbnail.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

image.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street rallies to record highs, oil up

children.content: The ASX 200 has lost ground, despite both the S&P 500 and tech-centric Nasdaq indexes ending last week with new record highs, while oil prices are surging with Brent crude above $US107 a barrel. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Two-hour barrier smashed twice in history-making London Marathon

children.content: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has become the first man to run a marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon with a time of 1:59:30.

image.altText: A runner in pink has his arms outstretched while crossing the finish line, world record time on a screen above his head.

items.title: What happens when Kitty Flanagan takes on Anne Edmonds's Bad Company

children.content: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

gallery.altText: Kitty Flanagan, left, crosses her arms next to Anne Edmonds, right, as both stand in a theatre.
Anne gesticulates wildly while speaking into a microphone behind a podium on stage, wearing a puffy dress.
Anne Edmonds and Kitty Flanagan look confused as they stand together in a theatre office.

thumbnail.altText: Kitty Flanagan, left, crosses her arms next to Anne Edmonds, right, as both stand in a theatre.
Anne gesticulates wildly while speaking into a microphone behind a podium on stage, wearing a puffy dress.
Anne Edmonds and Kitty Flanagan look confused as they stand together in a theatre office.

items.title: Why the bedroom is an 'invisible workplace' for women

children.content: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

image.altText: illustration of woman lying awake next to her sleeping partner

items.title: 'Angry nihilism': Why man behind 'Aussie' posters is bringing them back

children.content: Peter Drew has been hanging 'Aussie' posters in cities around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a big smile

items.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: When this mum's rental ended, she and her two teenagers moved into their car

children.content: An analysis of 12 months of urgent housing and homelessness attendances in Tasmania has shown an alarming spike in people with dependent children needing help — while overcrowding is an increasing concern.

image.altText: A mother with her head in her hands sits in the driver's seat of a car while two kids sit in the back under a blanket.

children.content: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

items.title: Influencers to pay $100 to make money off Tasmania's national parks

children.content: Influencers and content creators will be able to film and profit from content gathered in Tasmania's national parks for just $100, under new regulations.

items.title: Green on form as Knight Riders win IPL thriller

items.title: AI ball-tracking technology trialled in Darwin grade cricket

children.content: Darwin's premier women's cricket league becomes the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

items.title: Platypus researcher says the species is just 'hanging on' in NSW

children.content: A leading researcher is calling for the platypus to be recognised as a threatened species in NSW, saying a lack of data is the only reason it has not been reclassified.

items.title: Spanish derby descends into chaos after keeper punches opponent in face

items.title: The coastal RSL that traded gambling for a volunteer heartbeat

children.content: When its local RSL club was on the brink of collapse, a community on the NSW South Coast rallied a troop of volunteers to ensure it stayed open.

Coledale RSL as seen from the air with the Wollongong coastline and ocean further south.
